sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Cagliari, sitting at 14th in the league with 33 points, faces a formidable challenge against Fiorentina, who are positioned 8th with a strong 59 points in Round 33 of the Serie A at Unipol Domus in Cagliari. This matchup sees a struggling Cagliari side looking to climb out of the lower mid-table against a Fiorentina team aiming to solidify their upper-mid-table standing. The referee, Livio Marinelli, is known for a disciplinary approach with an average of 4.6 yellow cards per game and 0.29 red cards per game, which could influence the game's tempo.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Recent history heavily favors Fiorentina in this fixture, with the away side winning 4 of the last 5 matchups against Cagliari, while Cagliari have failed to secure a victory and managed just 1 draw. In their most recent encounter on December 8, 2024, Fiorentina clinched a narrow 1-0 victory at home, with Danilo Cataldi earning the Player of the Match title with a Sofascore rating of 7.9. Fiorentina have also maintained an unbeaten streak of 8 games against Cagliari, often scoring first in 4 of the last 5 meetings and leading at halftime in the same number of matches.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Fiorentina enter this match in impressive form, riding a 7-game unbeaten streak and frequently scoring over 2.5 goals in 7 of their last 9 matches. They have also been the first to score in 6 of their last 7 games, often leading at halftime in the same number of matches. Cagliari, on the other hand, have been more cautious, with matches often seeing less than 4.5 cards in their last 10 games and under 10.5 corners in 6 of their last 8 home games. Both teams share a consistent trend of matches finishing with fewer than 10.5 corners in their last 6 head-to-head encounters.

sections.subtitle: Cagliari's Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Cagliari will rely on forward Roberto Piccoli, who has scored 9 goals this season against an expected goals (xG) value of 10.76, showing a slight underperformance of -1.76. Defender Tommaso Augello stands out with a league rank of 2 for big chances created (12), alongside 6 assists and a strong average rating of 6.9. Midfielder Zortea Nadir has overperformed his xG by 3.00, scoring 6 goals from an expected 3.00, adding dynamism to Cagliari’s attack. However, Cagliari’s season stats reflect struggles, with 51 goals conceded (15th in the league for least goals conceded) and only 6 clean sheets (16th rank), though they rank 4th for headers scored with 9 goals.

sections.subtitle: Fiorentina's Standout Performers
sections.text: Fiorentina boast several key contributors, including midfielder Yacine Adli, who ranks 4th in the league for percentage of long balls in total passes (9.4%) and has scored 4 goals with 5 assists, holding an average rating of 7.07. Forward Albert Guðmundsson has overperformed his xG by 1.90, netting 6 goals, while defender Robin Gosens has impressed with 5 goals and 4 assists, boasting a rating of 7.08. Danilo Cataldi, a consistent performer against Cagliari with an average Sofascore rating of 7.5 across the last 5 matchups and 2 goals scored, will be crucial in midfield.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Team Stats
sections.text: Cagliari’s attack has shown some promise, ranking 7th in the league for big chances created (77) and penalty area entries per game (26.11), but their conversion rate is dismal at 25.97% (20th rank), missing 57 big chances (18th rank). Fiorentina’s goal distribution shows strength early in matches, with 28.57% of goals scored between the 46th and 60th minutes, while Cagliari peak late, with 23.53% of goals between the 46th and 60th minutes. Defensively, Cagliari commit many fouls (464, 7th rank) but have fewer yellow cards per game at 1.74 (6th rank for least yellows), which could play into referee Marinelli’s stricter style.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Venue Impact
sections.text: Cagliari have hit the woodwork 16 times this season, ranking 3rd in the league for unluckiest teams in this regard, which might haunt them again at Unipol Domus. Fiorentina have not kept a clean sheet against Cagliari in their last 5 away games in this fixture, suggesting Cagliari might find the net despite their struggles. Both teams rank well for corners, with Cagliari averaging 4.83 per game (7th rank) and a shared trend of low-corner games in head-to-heads, often under 10.5. Cagliari also excel in assisted goals, with 75% of their goals coming from assists (2nd in the league), showcasing their teamwork.
